meta.description: Canonical UTF-8 torture-corpus for cross-repo round-trip testing. Each entry exercises one realistic encoding hazard. Round-trip tests assert read(write(input)) == input. Normalization tests (WX-2) assert the *_form fields. Mojibake-fix tests assert to_storage_form(input) == expected_storage.

schema.input: Raw UTF-8 string the storage layer must round-trip losslessly.
schema.expected_storage: Canonical (mojibake-fixed) form. Equals input for non-mojibake categories.
schema.expected_match_form: Output of to_match_form(expected_storage). NFKD + strip combining marks + lowercase + Greek sigma fold + trim. Null when WX-2 has not formalized the rule for this script.
schema.expected_ascii_form: Output of to_ascii_form(expected_storage). Lowercase ASCII transliteration. Null for scripts without a defined transliteration in v1.
schema.notes: Why this entry exists; which encoding incident or hazard category it pins.

sources: WXYC/docs#6 (Mojibake Cleanup epic) — V012 round-trippable fix-pairs and V013 lossy rows
Real WXYC artist names from src/test-utils/wxyc-example-data.json where applicable

greek.notes: U+03C2 Greek lowercase final-form sigma. LML#168 sigma fold target — ς and σ are positional variants of the same letter and must collapse to σ in the match form.
U+03C3 Greek lowercase medial-form sigma. The canonical bucket that ς folds into.
U+03A3 Greek capital sigma. Lowercases to medial σ via default Unicode case mapping (no Greek-locale awareness in Rust char::to_lowercase).
Greek word ending in final-form sigma ς. Pinned acceptance: must hash to the same match-form bucket as Στελλάσ (medial-form ending). LML#168 canonical example.
Same Greek word ending in medial-form sigma. Match-form must equal the final-form variant above.
Real WXYC artist (Mike Paradinas / Planet Mu). Mixed Greek lowercase letter + ASCII; exercises mid-string non-Latin probing.
U+03A9 Greek capital omega. Single-character probe; case-mapping path.
Capital sigma followed by Latin letters — exercises mixed-script storage and is the input form for the mojibake_known Î£tella → Σtella fix-pair.

cyrillic.notes: Soviet rock band Kino — real WXYC repertoire. Cyrillic lowercase round-trip and basic transliteration.
Molchat Doma — real WXYC artist (Belarusian post-punk). Multi-word Cyrillic with whitespace.
U+0401 Cyrillic capital Yo — case-mapping path; transliteration that is not 1:1.
U+044B Cyrillic small yeru — single-char probe; no obvious Latin equivalent.
AukcYon (Auktsyon) — real Russian art-rock band on WXYC rotation.

cjk.notes: Toru Takemitsu — Japanese composer in WXYC classical/electronic repertoire. 3-byte UTF-8; no case mapping. ASCII transliteration is non-trivial (kanji readings depend on context); v1 does not commit.
Ryuichi Sakamoto — well-known WXYC artist. 4-character kanji string; tests multi-codepoint CJK passthrough.
Single CJK ideograph (V012 fix-pair coverage). 3-byte UTF-8 probe.
Single CJK ideograph (V012 fix-pair coverage). 3-byte UTF-8 probe.
Single CJK ideograph (V012 fix-pair coverage). 3-byte UTF-8 probe.

arabic.notes: Hamza Namira — Egyptian singer-songwriter. RTL multi-word Arabic; V012 fix-pair coverage. Transliteration not committed in v1.
Single Arabic letter dal. RTL single-char probe (V012).
Fairouz — Lebanese vocalist, real WXYC repertoire. Multi-letter Arabic, RTL.

hebrew.notes: Shiraz Tal — RTL multi-word Hebrew; tests bidi rendering and storage round-trip.
Single Hebrew letter shin — RTL single-char probe.

emoji.notes: U+1F44B waving hand — 4-byte UTF-8 (supplementary plane). Catches storage layers truncating to BMP.
Mixed ASCII + emoji — common DJ-comment pattern; tests 4-byte UTF-8 surviving alongside ASCII case-folding.

latin_extended.notes: Polish ł — does NOT decompose under NFKD (it has no combining-mark equivalent). Survives as-is through normalize_artist_name; transliteration to 'l' is a separate ascii_form rule.
Real WXYC artist. ö (U+00F6) decomposes to o + combining diaeresis under NFKD.
Canonical WXYC artist. Spanish é (U+00E9) NFKD path.
Canonical WXYC artist (Hungarian). ó (U+00F3) NFKD path.
Canonical WXYC artist. Turkish ü (U+00FC) NFKD path.
Capital É (U+00C9) — NFKD decompose + lowercase. The Édith vs Edith equivalence-class case from WXYC/docs#6 P3.
Canonical WXYC artist. Multi-diacritic Turkish: ş (decomposes) + ı (does NOT decompose under NFKD, dotless-i). Match form keeps ı; ascii_form transliterates.
Real WXYC artist (Icelandic). ó (U+00F3) NFKD path.

bidi_marks.notes: U+200E LEFT-TO-RIGHT MARK embedded in ASCII. LRM is category Cf (Format), NOT M (Mark) — it is NOT stripped by NFKD + is_mark filter. Match-form behavior is undefined in v1 (WX-2 must decide whether to strip Cf characters).
U+200F RIGHT-TO-LEFT MARK. Same Cf-not-stripped hazard as LRM. Invisible character that survives most pipelines and corrupts visual rendering later.
U+202E RIGHT-TO-LEFT OVERRIDE + U+202C POP DIRECTIONAL FORMATTING. Spoofing-attack vector; must round-trip but should ideally be flagged on storage (out of scope for WX-1).

zwj.notes: Family-of-four ZWJ sequence (man + ZWJ + woman + ZWJ + girl + ZWJ + boy). 7 codepoints, 25 UTF-8 bytes. Tests storage layers that count graphemes vs codepoints vs bytes.
Regional indicator pair (U+1F1FA + U+1F1F8) rendered as 🇺🇸 flag. Two supplementary-plane codepoints that visually combine.
Singer ZWJ sequence (man + ZWJ + microphone). Music-relevant; tests mid-emoji-sequence ZWJ U+200D survival.

normalization.notes: NFC form: U+0063 U+0061 U+0066 U+00E9. Storage MUST preserve as-is (no silent re-normalization). Match-form folds to bare ASCII via NFKD + strip combining.
NFD form of café: U+0063 U+0061 U+0066 U+0065 U+0301. Visually identical to the NFC entry above; bytes differ. Both must round-trip; both must produce the same match form.
NFC form: U+00F1. Single precomposed Spanish ñ.
NFD form of ñ: U+006E + U+0303. Bytes differ from NFC entry; match form must collide.

mojibake_known.notes: UTF-8 capital sigma 0xCE 0xA3 misread as Latin-1 → Î (0xCE) + £ (0xA3). V012 round-trippable fix-pair. WX-2's to_storage_form must reverse this.
UTF-8 é (0xC3 0xA9) misread as Latin-1 → Ã (0xC3) + © (0xA9). The single most common Latin-1-as-UTF-8 mojibake pattern.
UTF-8 ñ (0xC3 0xB1) misread as Latin-1 → Ã (0xC3) + ± (0xB1). V012 fix-pair.
UTF-8 ö (0xC3 0xB6) misread as Latin-1 → Ã (0xC3) + ¶ (0xB6). V012 fix-pair.
UTF-8 ü (0xC3 0xBC) misread as Latin-1 → Ã (0xC3) + ¼ (0xBC). V012 fix-pair.
UTF-8 right single quote U+2019 (0xE2 0x80 0x99) misread as Windows-1252 → â (0xE2) + € (0x80) + ™ (0x99). The smart-quote mojibake. V013 lossy territory.

quoting.notes: ASCII apostrophe U+0027 — exercises SQL string quoting; charset bugs often co-occur with quoting bugs.
ASCII double-quote U+0022 — JSON / CSV / SQL escape boundary.
ASCII backslash U+005C — JSON / SQL / shell escape boundary.
ASCII semicolon — SQL statement terminator hazard.
Embedded NUL byte (U+0000). SQLite TEXT permits, PostgreSQL TEXT does NOT (server raises 22021). Match/ascii forms undefined — WX-2 may reject or strip.
Embedded TAB (U+0009). TSV / CSV column-boundary hazard; many readers split on tab silently.
